exemplar.title: Exploring the Impact of GIR on Scoring Performance in the Golf PGA Tour

detailedExemplarData.title: Exploring the Impact of GIR on Scoring Performance in the Golf PGA Tour

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear logical progression from introduction through conclusion
Well-structured table of contents, numbered headings, and embedded figures
Mostly concise and relevant writing with purposeful links to the research question
weaknesses: Minor formatting lapses in bibliography and table of contents indentation
Occasional verbosity in definitions and small diversions into non-essential terminology
Incomplete hypothesis statement that interrupts initial development
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration exhibits a coherent and sustained logical flow (Band 3) with a clear introduction, body, and conclusion structure. Transitions between statistical methods are explicit, and figures/tables are integrated well. However, repeated definitions, citation inconsistencies, and formatting artefacts prevent it from reaching Band 4, so a score of 3 is warranted.

strengths: Appropriate use of key symbols (r, Ï, Î¼, xÌ, df) with clear variable definitions
Effective combination of tables, scatter plots, and Excel output to illustrate results
Explanations accompany symbols and figures, yielding overall clear communication
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (missing multiplication dots, mis-rendered Greek letters)
Varying units (percent vs decimal) and inconsistent significant-figure use
Lack of advanced representations (residual plots, confidence intervals) to reach the highest band
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are largely correct and accompanied by effective representations (Band 3). Clarity is mostly consistent, though minor notational glitches and unit inconsistencies prevent a higher mark, so the student receives 3 out of 4.

strengths: Clear personal motivation linked to childhood exposure to golf
Independent development of a bespoke sampling plan and classification rules
Demonstrated initiative in assembling and analyzing an 85-player dataset
weaknesses: Procedures follow conventional textbook methods without novel extensions
Selection rationale (e.g., median player choice) could be better justified
No truly original model formulation or innovative graphic design
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Significant evidence of independent thinking and a clear personal approach drive the exploration, matching Band 2 descriptors. While the dataset and plan reflect initiative, the mathematical procedures remain conventional, so a mark of 2 is appropriate.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on correlation values, p-values, and slope interpretations
Explicit critique of limitations such as omitted variables and dataset size
Clear links between findings and enhanced understanding of golf performance
weaknesses: Limited depth in statistical critique (no assumption checks, effect-size discussion)
Reflection lacks in-depth model diagnostics (e.g., heteroscedasticity, non-linearity)
Opportunities for richer insights (prediction intervals, residual analysis) are not fully explored
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful reflection and some critical analysis throughout (Band 2). They articulate clear connections to their understanding, but the absence of deeper statistical interrogation and formal assumption checks prevents Band 3, justifying a score of 2.

strengths: Purposeful application of multiple HL-level statistical methods (PMCC, regression, t-test)
Accurate computations with only minor rounding discrepancies
Sound conceptual explanations accompanying each method demonstrating rigour
weaknesses: Lacks formal assumption checks (e.g., Leveneâs test, normality diagnostics)
No confidence or prediction intervals to assess model uncertainty
Minor misstatements in unit interpretation of slope and correlation hypotheses
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is used effectively and goes beyond basic course-level techniques by integrating several HL methods in an authentic context. Accuracy is high and explanations show rigour, warranting a high mark. Though not fully âthorough with rigourâ (missing formal diagnostics and intervals), the sophisticated combination of methods justifies elevating the score to 5 out of 6.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing a personally meaningful sport and deciding to test multiple statistical techniques shows noticeable independent thinking beyond a canned example.  Nevertheless, the mathematics follows textbook procedures without original extensions, so the evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

Explanations accompanying each statistical method reveal sound conceptual graspâe.g., interpreting negative slope in golf context, explaining rationale for unequal-variance t-test.  Understanding is evident throughout but lacks the depth (e.g. formal assumptions checks, confidence interval interpretation) that characterises âthorough with rigourâ bands 5-6.

The calculated r, regression coefficients, p-values, and t-statistics are internally consistent with the provided sums; minor rounding discrepancies are the only noted issues.  Thus the mathematics is largely correct with only minor errors, satisfying band 4.

Most writing is purposeful and related to the research question.  Occasional redundancy (e.g. extended explanations of tee, fairway, green; duplicated reference list entries) and some verbose sentences reduce conciseness, yet very little is truly irrelevant.  Hence the work is ârelevant and mostly conciseâ rather than uniformly succinct.

Key symbols (r, Ï, Î¼, xÌ, df, PMCC formula) are used correctly and variables are defined.  A few notational glitches (missing multiplication dots, mis-rendered Greek letters in the table) prevent full consistency, but mathematical language is largely appropriate throughout.

Explanations generally accompany symbols and figures, producing clear communication.  Minor inconsistenciesâsuch as switching between percentage and decimal for GIR or varying significant-figure useâstop the work just short of complete consistency, yet overall clarity is maintained.

Pearson correlation, linear regression, expected-value predictions, and a two-sample t-test are solid, relevant HL-level statistics applied to an authentic dataset.  The mathematics is used purposefully to address the question, fulfilling the descriptor for âused effectivelyâ but not venturing into more sophisticated or multivariate techniques that would merit bands 5-6.

The student employs multiple representations effectively: labelled data tables, a scatter plot with regression line and equation, numerical output tables from Excel, and summary statistics.  These representations are well chosen for the statistical techniques used but do not reach the sophistication (e.g. residual plots, confidence intervals visualised) associated with the highest band.

Throughout analysis and conclusion the student comments on the meaning of correlation values, slopes, p-values, and acknowledges other performance factors, demonstrating meaningful reflection.  Reflection is present but not probing enough (e.g. no discussion of effect size or model diagnostics) for the highest band.

Initiative is evident in assembling a fresh 85-player dataset, constructing classification rules (quartiles), and running several statistical tests in Excel.  Creativity is adequate yet conventional; there is no novel model formulation or innovative graphic, fitting âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ creativity.

The exploration moves logically from introduction, objectives, methodology, analysis, to conclusion, and each statistical technique is introduced before it is applied.  The reader is seldom left wondering *why* a step is taken, indicating clear causal links between sections.  Minor lapsesâe.g. repeating the definition of GIR and small diversions into golf terminologyâslightly interrupt the flow, so the work does not quite reach the âexcellentâ descriptor, but overall coherence is strong and sustained.

There is explicit critique of limitationsâmoderate correlation strength, omitted variables, dataset sizeâshowing some critical analysis.  The critique stops short of an in-depth statistical evaluation (e.g., heteroscedasticity, non-linearity checks), so the analysis is meaningful but not âdeepâ.

Links between mathematical findings and the studentâs understanding of golf performance are articulated (e.g. translating slope to strokes saved, relating negative r to better scores).  These links clearly enhance understanding, yet they are not expanded with richer insights that would warrant the top band.

A table of contents, numbered headings, and separate appendix give the text a recognisable introduction-body-conclusion structure.  Figures and tables are embedded close to commentary, and the bibliography is placed after conclusions.  Transitions between sections (e.g. PMCC â regression â t-test) are explicit, though page-break artefacts from the export make the layout slightly less seamless than âexceptionalâ organization.

The studentâs narrativeâlinking childhood exposure to golf, formulating a bespoke sampling plan, and selecting specific players for deeper comparisonâdemonstrates a clear personal approach that drives the exploration, but the investigation would not collapse without these touches, hence band 2.

annotations.comment: Your criteria for selecting high, median, and low GIR players is stated, but explain why median selection uses a single player rather than a group.
The title is clear and directly reflects the research focus, providing strong initial engagement.
The table of contents entries are misaligned and lack consistent indentation. Clean up formatting to improve navigation.
The detailed definitions of tee, fairway, and green are informative but verbose. Condense these explanations to maintain relevance.
The introductory hypothesis statement trails off mid-sentence, leaving the research question underdeveloped. Complete the clause for clarity.
Objectives are clearly numbered and directly support the research aim, demonstrating sound structural planning.
The description of systematic sampling lacks detail on interval selection and potential bias reduction. Specify sampling procedure steps.
The player data table is comprehensive, but a brief note on how players were ordered or selected would aid interpretation.
The PMCC equation is correctly stated but misses multiplication dots and consistent notation for Greek letters. Standardize symbols.
The summation values for \\Sigma x, \\Sigma y, etc. are listed but lack units or context. Identify what units are used for each variable.
Substitution into the PMCC formula is shown clearly, but rounding at intermediate steps can distort the final r. Verify significant figures.
The hypotheses use \rho for correlation but your regression test uses \beta and \rho. Clarify whether you are testing slope or correlation.
The regression output table retains raw quotes and generic labels (âTitleâ, âRegEqnâ). Clean formatting and label cells meaningfully.
The scatter plot effectively visualizes the relationship but would benefit from axis labels showing units (percent vs fraction) and a residual plot.
When interpreting the slope, clarify that x is a proportion (0â1) so a 1-unit change is 100%. Adjust language to reflect per-percentage change.
The discussion of the y-intercept notes its lack of practical meaning but misses the context of scale. Relate intercept to model boundaries.
The expected value section clearly applies the regression equation; consider computing prediction intervals to assess model uncertainty.
The expected vs actual results table is informative; including statistics on residual distribution (mean, variance) would deepen the analysis.
Future research suggestions are well conceived, demonstrating critical reflection on the scope and applicability of this investigation.
The bibliography contains duplicated entries and inconsistent citation formatting. Standardize reference style (e.g., APA) for consistency.
The interpretation of differences notes additional factors but could be strengthened by quantifying effect sizes of those omitted variables.
The two-sample t-test table uses quotes around labels and raw Excel output. Reformat for clarity and align headings with hypotheses.
The narrative around the unequal-variance assumption is good; add a reference to Leveneâs test or sample variance ratio to justify this choice.
The conclusion correctly summarizes findings but misstates the slope impact as â0.1076 strokesâ rather than 0.1076 strokes per 1% change; clarify unit scale.
The acknowledgment of limitations is appropriate; extend reflection by discussing assumption checks (normality, homoscedasticity) for tests used.
